# anypoint_private_space_vpn (Data Source)

Reads a single CloudHub 2.0 private-space VPN connection by its connection id. Returns every member of the connection along with their tunnels. Note that `psk` is never returned by the Anypoint API (always empty) and `startup_action` reflects the API's stale GET value (see the `anypoint_private_space_vpn` resource for context).

## Example Usage

```terraform
###############################################################################
# anypoint_private_space_vpn (singular)
#
# Read one VPN connection by its connection_id. Use this when you need to
# reference an existing VPN connection (created out of band or by another
# stack) from inside your terraform configuration.
###############################################################################

data "anypoint_private_space_vpn" "this" {
  org_id           = var.root_org
  private_space_id = var.private_space_id
  connection_id    = var.connection_id
}

# Connection-level fields.
output "vpn_connection_name" {
  value = data.anypoint_private_space_vpn.this.name
}

# Per-member fields. Each member exposes connection_name, local_asn,
# remote_asn, remote_ip_address, static_routes, vpn_id, vpn_connection_status
# and the nested vpn_tunnels block (psk is masked).
output "vpn_member_ids" {
  value = [for v in data.anypoint_private_space_vpn.this.vpns : v.vpn_id]
}

output "vpn_first_member_status" {
  description = "Lifecycle status of the first VPN member (unavailable / pending / available)."
  value       = try(data.anypoint_private_space_vpn.this.vpns[0].vpn_connection_status, null)
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `connection_id` (String) The VPN connection id (GUID).
- `org_id` (String) The organization id where the private space lives.
- `private_space_id` (String) The private space id.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `name` (String) Display name of the VPN connection.
- `vpns` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--vpns))

<a id="nestedatt--vpns"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vpns`

Read-Only:

- `connection_id` (String)
- `connection_name` (String)
- `local_asn` (String)
- `name` (String)
- `remote_asn` (String)
- `remote_ip_address` (String)
- `startup_action` (String)
- `static_routes` (List of String)
- `vpn_connection_status` (String)
- `vpn_id` (String)
- `vpn_tunnels` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedobjatt--vpns--vpn_tunnels))

<a id="nestedobjatt--vpns--vpn_tunnels"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vpns.vpn_tunnels`

Read-Only:

- `is_logs_enabled` (Boolean)
- `psk` (String)
- `ptp_cidr` (String)
